skill.name: skill-from-masters
skill.description: Create better AI skills by grounding them in proven expert methodologies before using skill-creator.
skill.summary: Research expert methods, select strong frameworks, and prepare better inputs for skill creation.
skill.author: GBSOSS

security_audit.summary: Static weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and external command alerts were reviewed as false positives caused by prose, Markdown formatting, and framework names. The only confirmed concern is legitimate web research behavior, which may send user-provided topics to search or browsing tools.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain methodology names, expert names, book titles, and prose. I found no cryptographic APIs, hash functions, encryption routines, or credential handling at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is plain Markdown reference material. There is no executable code or cryptographic operation to misuse.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Research Text
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe questions, web research checks, and framework references. They do not enumerate local systems, inspect host configuration, or execute discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear in skill workflow prose and a methodology table. I found no command examples such as host, user, process, or network enumeration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Mistaken For Command Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged external command location is a Markdown-formatted file path reference. It tells the assistant to read a local reference document and does not invoke Ruby, shell, or subprocess execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only inline Markdown code formatting around references/methodology-database.md. There is no executable syntax or user-controlled command string.

low_findings.title: Skill Uses External Web Research
low_findings.description: The workflow instructs the assistant to search the web and fetch primary sources. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but users should avoid including confidential topics in research queries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly asks for web searches and source fetching. The behavior is intentional and bounded to research rather than data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Many new AI skills start from generic advice and miss proven domain practice. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code research expert methods before generating a skill.
actual_capabilities: Maps a proposed skill idea to relevant professional domains.
Uses a local methodology database of experts, frameworks, and source types.
Guides web research for additional experts, primary sources, and golden examples.
Helps users choose one to three methodologies for a skill foundation.
Extracts principles, processes, quality criteria, and anti-patterns before skill generation.
Hands the selected methodology package to skill-creator for final skill creation.
limitations: It depends on web search quality when local references are not enough.
It does not generate the final skill by itself; it prepares input for skill-creator.
It may require user judgment when expert frameworks conflict.
It does not verify paid books or unavailable primary sources directly.

use_cases.title: Create a Domain Skill
use_cases.description: Identify expert frameworks before building a new skill for sales, design, product, engineering, or writing.

use_cases.title: Upgrade a Generic Skill Idea
use_cases.description: Turn a broad skill concept into a structured methodology with source-backed principles and quality checks.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skill Design
use_cases.description: Use a repeatable research workflow so team skills reflect credible sources and consistent evaluation criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a skill for writing customer onboarding emails. Find relevant expert methods first, then recommend the best foundation.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Methodologies
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a skill for product discovery interviews. Compare expert methodologies and help me choose one to three approaches.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Research Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Research expert methods for engineering code review. Include principles, process steps, quality criteria, pitfalls, and example outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Multiple Domains
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the methodology foundation for a strategy memo skill that combines business writing, decision making, and executive communication.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for product requirement documents.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies product management and business writing as core domains, compares Cagan, Torres, Minto, and Amazon methods, then recommends a problem-first narrative structure.

output_examples.input: Make a skill for better user interviews.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes The Mom Test, Interviewing Users, and Jobs to be Done, then extracts question rules, rapport practices, and common mistakes.

output_examples.input: Prepare a skill for sales discovery calls.
output_examples.output: The skill maps the request to sales and persuasion, reviews SPIN Selling and Challenger Sale, and produces a source-backed checklist for skill-creator.

best_practices: Start with a clear skill task, target user, and quality bar.
Prefer primary sources, respected books, and practitioner talks over shallow summaries.
Ask the user to approve the selected methodologies before invoking skill-creator.
anti_patterns: Do not create a skill from generic advice without checking expert methods.
Do not combine conflicting frameworks without explaining the synthesis.
Do not treat the local database as complete when a domain needs current research.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It researches expert methodologies and prepares a stronger foundation for creating a new AI skill.

faq.question: Does it replace skill-creator?
faq.answer: No. It runs before skill-creator and improves the methodology used in the final skill.

faq.question: Does it require web access?
faq.answer: Web access is useful because the workflow asks for expert research, primary sources, and golden examples.

faq.question: Which domains are included locally?
faq.answer: The reference file includes writing, product, design, sales, leadership, engineering, startups, productivity, negotiation, and finance.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users provide first?
faq.answer: Users should provide the skill goal, target tasks, target audience, and desired quality level.
